UAT-PM-001 Create and Publish a Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO) end-to-end SIGNED OFF в–ј
Tester
J. Torres
Date Executed
Feb 21, 2026
Related Story
GRAN-37
Overall Result
PASS
1
Log in to DGLMS as Program Manager ([email protected])
Expected: Dashboard loads with Program Manager role permissions. Sidebar shows NOFO Management, Grant Lifecycle, Reports.
вң“ Result: Dashboard loaded correctly. All PM menu items visible.
2
Navigate to NOFO Management вҶ’ Click "Create New NOFO"
Expected: New NOFO record form opens with required fields highlighted. ALN field auto-populates from SAM.gov.
вң“ Result: Form opened. ALN populated from GRAN-104 SAM.gov integration.
3
Complete all required NOFO fields: Title, Program Area, Funding Amount, Eligibility Criteria, Application Deadline
Expected: All required fields accept input. Funding amount validates as numeric. Deadline enforces future date.
вң“ Result: All validations passed. Date picker enforced future-only selection.
4
Attach supporting document (PDF, <10MB) using the document attachment portlet
Expected: File uploads successfully. Document appears in attachment list. Audit trail records "Document Added" event.
вң“ Result: Document uploaded. GRAN-106 functionality confirmed. Audit event logged.
5
Submit NOFO for Approval вҖ” click "Submit for Review"
Expected: Status changes to "Pending Review". NOFO routed to approver queue. PM receives email confirmation. Edit button disabled.
вң“ Result: Status updated. Approver notified. Edit locked. TC-GRAN-105-002 edge case noted as separate defect.
вң“ UAT Sign-Off вҖ” J. Torres, DOT Program Manager
Scenario completed successfully. NOFO creation workflow matches business requirements. Signed off Feb 21, 2026.

1
Log in and navigate to an existing NOFO in "Pending Review" status
вң“ Pass
2
Attempt to edit the Program Area field вҖ” should be locked
вң“ Pass вҖ” field correctly locked
3
Attach a new document while status = Pending Review
Expected: Attachment allowed. Conditional validation re-runs. Status remains Pending Review.
вң— FAIL вҖ” Attaching document resets status field. BUG-042 logged.

Expected: Record opens in read-only view
2

Expected: Complete history of field changes with timestamp, user, old value, new value
3
Export history as PDF for audit documentation
Expected: PDF generates with all history entries, DOT logo header, timestamp

Meeting Notes
Sprint standups В· DOT sessions В· Decision log
Feb 25, 2026 В· 10:00 AM EST В· Sprint 6 Mid-Sprint Check-in
Sprint 6 Mid-Sprint Review вҖ” GRAN-105 Critical Issues
Critical DiscussionBUG-042BUG-043

Attendees: Ana Llorens, Dev Team Lead, J. Torres (DOT PM)

Discussion focused on the two critical defects blocking GRAN-105 UAT sign-off. Dev team presented root cause analysis: the conditional field trigger fires before the document attachment event completes, causing a race condition. Fix estimated at 2вҖ“3 days.

Action Items
вҶ’
Dev Team: Fix trigger order for GRAN-105 by Feb 28 В· Critical
вҶ’
Ana (AL): Create regression test cases for GRAN-105 fix В· Due Mar 1
вҶ’
J. Torres: Communicate UAT timeline slip to DOT stakeholders В· Due Feb 26
Decisions: GRAN-105 UAT sign-off target moved from Feb 28 вҶ’ Mar 5 pending defect resolution.
Feb 21, 2026 В· 09:00 AM EST В· UAT Session
UAT Session 1 вҖ” Program Manager + Approver Personas
2 Sign-OffsUAT

Attendees: Ana Llorens, J. Torres (PM), M. Reyes (Approver), R. Diaz (Grants Officer)

Executed UAT-PM-001 (NOFO creation), UAT-AP-001 (approval chain), UAT-GO-001 (document management). All three passed. J. Torres and M. Reyes provided formal sign-off. UAT-PM-003 (conditional fields) escalated as failing вҖ” BUG-042 logged during session.

вҶ’

вҶ’
AL: File BUG-042 and BUG-043 in defect tracker В· Done вң“

Committed to Sprint 6 stories: GRAN-104, GRAN-105, GRAN-106, GRAN-110, GRAN-111. UAT Phase 1 scheduled for Feb 21. Team velocity from S5: 38 pts. S6 capacity: 41 pts.

Scope of Work / PWS
Performance Work Statement В· DOT DGLMS В· Contract Reference
в„№ This document reflects the Performance Work Statement (PWS) scope as applicable to PHSU/THI's implementation responsibilities for the DOT Digital Grant Lifecycle Management System.
В§ 1Background and Purposeв–ј

The Department of Transportation (DOT) requires a modern, cloud-based Digital Grant Lifecycle Management System (DGLMS) to streamline the full lifecycle of federal grant programs вҖ” from Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO) publication through award, monitoring, and close-out. The DGLMS replaces legacy manual processes and disparate systems with a unified Salesforce-based platform that meets federal security and accessibility standards.

PHSU/THI serves as the implementing contractor responsible for Salesforce configuration, custom development, testing, compliance validation, and system documentation in support of DOT's ATO (Authority to Operate) objectives.

Contracting AgencyU.S. Department of Transportation (DOT) Implementing PartnerPonce Health Sciences University / THI (PHSU/THI) PlatformSalesforce Government Cloud Plus ATO TargetQ3 2026 (FedRAMP Moderate) Contract TypeTime & Materials (T&M) with Deliverable Milestones
В§ 2System Overview and Scopeв–ј

The DGLMS encompasses the following core functional modules, all implemented within Salesforce and subject to federal compliance requirements:

ModuleDescriptionGRAN StoriesStatus
Grant Lifecycle ManagementEnd-to-end tracking from NOFO creation through award and close-out. Includes status workflows, conditional field logic, and version control.GRAN-25, GRAN-37, GRAN-105, GRAN-110In Progress
Funding Opportunity (NOFO) ManagementCreation, editing, approval routing, and publication of Notices of Funding Opportunity. Integrates with ALN (Assistance Listing Number) from SAM.gov.GRAN-37, GRAN-104Functional
Document ManagementUpload, versioning, and association of supporting documents to grant records. Enforces file type restrictions and max version limits.GRAN-27, GRAN-81, GRAN-106, GRAN-110Functional
Approval WorkflowsRole-based routing of NOFO packages through DOT approval chains. Includes digital signature capture and audit logging.GRAN-37, GRAN-38Functional
External IntegrationReal-time integration with SAM.gov (ALN validation) and Grants.gov (package submission and status sync).GRAN-39, GRAN-104Functional
Reporting and AnalyticsPDF report generation for grant records, executive dashboards, and audit trail exports.GRAN-42Functional
User EntitlementsRole-based access control aligned to DOT organizational hierarchy. Profiles: Program Manager, Grants Officer, Approver, External Applicant, System Admin.GRAN-25, GRAN-26, GRAN-28, GRAN-29Functional
Collaboration (Chatter)Role-scoped Salesforce Chatter portlet for internal grant team communication. Not visible to external applicants.GRAN-28, GRAN-29Functional
В§ 3Technical Requirementsв–ј

В§ 3.1 Platform: The system shall be implemented on Salesforce Government Cloud Plus, which provides FedRAMP High authorization at the infrastructure level. Custom configurations, Apex code, and LWC components are subject to DOT configuration management and security review.

В§ 3.2 Access Control: The system shall enforce least-privilege role-based access control (RBAC) through Salesforce Profiles, Permission Sets, and Sharing Rules. All access changes shall be logged with timestamp and user attribution. Specific requirements: (1) No user shall access grant data outside their assigned program area; (2) Edit access shall be revoked upon status change to "Submitted"; (3) All role changes require administrator approval and audit entry.

В§ 3.3 External Integrations: The system shall maintain real-time integration with (1) SAM.gov API for ALN validation with <3 second response time SLA; (2) Grants.gov for package submission with synchronous status confirmation; (3) DOT's internal identity provider for SSO authentication (IA-2).

В§ 3.4 Audit and Logging: All create, read-sensitive, update, and delete (CRUD) operations shall generate audit log entries per NIST 800-53 AU-2 and AU-12. Audit logs shall be immutable, timestamped, and retain for a minimum of 3 years.

В§ 3.5 Reporting: The system shall provide PDF export capability for all NOFO records and audit trails. Reports shall include DOT letterhead, classification markings, and generation timestamp.

В§ 4Compliance Requirementsв–ј

В§ 4.1 Section 508 / WCAG 2.1 AA: The system shall conform to Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act (29 U.S.C. В§ 794d) and WCAG 2.1 Level AA success criteria. A Voluntary Product Accessibility Template (VPAT) shall be produced prior to ATO. All UI components shall be keyboard-navigable, support screen readers (JAWS, NVDA), and maintain minimum 4.5:1 color contrast ratio.

В§ 4.2 FedRAMP Moderate: The system shall achieve and maintain FedRAMP Moderate authorization. The System Security Plan (SSP) shall document implementation of all applicable NIST 800-53 Rev. 5 controls. A Third-Party Assessment Organization (3PAO) shall conduct an independent security assessment prior to ATO decision.

В§ 4.3 NIST 800-53 Rev. 5 Key Control Families:

Control FamilyKey ControlsImplementation Approach
Access Control (AC)AC-2, AC-3, AC-6, AC-17Salesforce Profiles, Permission Sets, Sharing Rules, MFA
Audit & Accountability (AU)AU-2, AU-9, AU-12Field History Tracking, Setup Audit Trail, Event Monitoring
Configuration Mgmt (CM)CM-3, CM-6, CM-7Version control, change management process, approval gates
Identification & Auth (IA)IA-2, IA-5, IA-8DOT SSO integration, MFA enforcement, session management
System & Comm Protection (SC)SC-5, SC-8, SC-28HTTPS/TLS 1.3, DoS protection, data encryption at rest
System & Info Integrity (SI)SI-3, SI-10, SI-12Input validation, malware protection, data retention controls
В§ 5Deliverables and Performance Standardsв–ј
DeliverableDescriptionDueStatus
D-001: Test PlanComprehensive test plan covering system, integration, UAT, 508, and performance testingSprint 3Delivered
D-002: Test Case Suite32+ individual test cases in Jira-compatible format with NIST control mappingSprint 5Delivered
D-003: UAT Scripts12 persona-based UAT scripts with step-by-step execution and sign-off documentationSprint 5Delivered
D-004: UAT Sign-Off PackageCompleted UAT results with business stakeholder sign-offs for all 12 scenariosSprint 73/12 Complete
D-005: Section 508 VPATVoluntary Product Accessibility Template documenting 508 compliance statusSprint 8Not Started
D-006: System Security Plan (SSP)Complete SSP per FedRAMP Moderate template with all control implementations documentedSprint 8Not Started
D-007: 3PAO Assessment ReportIndependent security assessment findings and recommendation for ATOMay 2026Pending Vendor
D-008: Plan of Action & Milestones (POA&M)Documented remediation plan for any findings from 3PAO assessmentJun 2026Not Started
D-009: ATO Package SubmissionComplete ATO package submitted to Authorizing OfficialQ2 2026Planned
В§ 6Testing Requirementsв–ј

В§ 6.1 Testing Phases: The following testing phases are required prior to ATO:

PhaseTypeExecutorAcceptance Criteria
System TestingFunctional, Security, IntegrationPHSU/THI (AL)100% of Critical/High test cases pass; no open Critical defects
User Acceptance TestingBusiness workflow validationDOT Business UsersAll 12 UAT scripts executed; 100% signed off by authorized DOT personnel
Section 508 TestingAccessibility conformancePHSU/THI + 508 specialistWCAG 2.1 AA pass; VPAT completed; no Critical 508 defects
Performance TestingLoad, stress, volumePHSU/THISystem supports 50+ concurrent users; <3s response time at 80th percentile
Security Assessment (3PAO)Independent penetration + control testingThird-Party AssessorAll POA&M items documented; no P1 findings unmitigated

В§ 6.2 Defect Resolution: Critical severity defects shall be resolved within 5 business days of identification. High severity within 10 business days. All defects shall be tracked in the project defect management system (this portal) with resolution evidence prior to final sign-off.

DOT DGLMS В· Sprint 6 Executive Summary
Executive Summary вҖ” Sprint 6

DGLMS is in Sprint 6 of 8 with UAT Phase 1 underway. Test pass rate: 75% (24/32).

вҡ  Critical: GRAN-105 has 2 critical defects (BUG-042, 043) blocking UAT. Dev target: Feb 28.

Section 508: 60% вҖ” BUG-040 (Firefox) high priority. GRAN-113 in sprint.

On track: SAM.gov (GRAN-104), NOFO approvals (GRAN-37), field history, PDF export all complete with sign-offs.

How this works
Each row in a persona tab = one complete test script for that persona.
The same test ID on Grant Admin and OST Admin tabs = two separate scripts вҖ” not duplicates.
Imported scripts appear in Test Cases and are executable per persona.
Columns read: Test Case #, Testing type, Title, Test Scenario, Related Story, Prerequisites, Testing Instructions, Expected Results
